info.title: MICROSERVICE_DEV_Employee Directory Access
info.description: This API endpoint allows users to access the employee directory, providing information on employees within the organization. It serves as a resource for retrieving employee details, such as names, roles, and contact information. Typical consumers include internal tools, HR applications, and any systems requiring employee data for various purposes. By standardizing access to employee information, it enhances operational efficiency and supports better decision-making within teams.

get.description: This endpoint allows you to fetch all employee records from the system. You can use this to find information about various employees.

requestBody.description: This request retrieves a list of employees. You can specify query parameters to filter the results, but no additional body content is necessary for this request.

404.description: The requested employee resource was not found.

post.description: This endpoint allows you to create a new employee record in the system. Provide the necessary employee details in the request body.

requestBody.description: This request is used to create a new employee record. The body should contain the necessary details of the employee to be added in JSON format.

404.description: No matching employee resource was found to operate on.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_getEmployeesList-GetEmp.title: Employee Operation Error Response

employeeId.description: The unique identifier of the employee.

employeeCode.description: Indicates if there is a problem with the employee code.

message.description: Descriptive error message regarding the operation.
message.example: Employee created successfully and assigned to the Engineering department.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_getEmployeesList-GetEmp.description: Schema representing the error response when an employee operation fails.

example.message: Employee created successfully and assigned to the Engineering department.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_getEmployeesList-Employ.title: Employee Details

firstName.description: The first name of the employee.

lastName.description: The last name of the employee.

email.description: The email address of the employee.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the employee.

designation.description: The job designation of the employee.

employmentType.description: The type of employment (e.g., full-time, part-time).

joiningDate.description: The date when the employee joined the company.

managerId.description: The unique identifier of the employee's manager.

workLocation.description: The location where the employee works.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_getEmployeesList-Employ.description: Schema representing the personal details of an employee.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_getEmployeesList-Depart.title: Department Details

departmentId.description: The unique identifier of the department.

businessUnit.description: The business unit to which the department belongs.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_getEmployeesList-Depart.description: Schema representing the details of an employee's department.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_getEmployeesList-Access.title: Access Details

employeePortal.description: Indicates if the employee has access to the employee portal.

vpn.description: Indicates if the employee has VPN access.

emailProvisioning.description: Indicates if the employee has email provisioning.

laptopAssigned.description: Indicates if the employee has been assigned a laptop.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_getEmployeesList-Access.description: Schema representing the access rights of an employee.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_getEmployeesList-System.title: System Details

employeeUuid.description: The unique UUID assigned to the employee.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_getEmployeesList-System.description: Schema representing the system-related information of an employee.

category.description: Indicates whether the category is active.

customer.description: Details about the customer associated with the subscription.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_getEmployeesList-GetEmp.description: Request body schema for creating a new employee operation. Contains preferences for subscription, notifications, and customer details.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_getEmployeesList-GetEmp.title: ErrorResponse

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the error occurred.

status.description: The HTTP status code of the error.

error.description: A short description of the error.

message.description: A detailed message about the error and potential causes.
message.example: The requested portfolio PORT-99999 does not exist or has been removed.

path.description: The API path that was requested when the error occurred.

errorCode.description: A unique code for identifying the specific error type.

suggestions.description: An array of suggestions to help resolve the error.
example: Ensure the customer has an active portfolio
Contact support if the issue persists

items.example: Check the employee ID and try again.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_getEmployeesList-GetEmp.description: This schema represents the structure of an error response for the employee operations when a 404 error occurs.

example.message: The requested portfolio PORT-99999 does not exist or has been removed.
suggestions: Ensure the customer has an active portfolio
Contact support if the issue persists
